user_query:
I need to classify and prioritize qualitative feedback for a study. Please use the local assets in this workspace and produce three outputs.

Read input/transcripts_round1.jsonl and input/transcripts_round2.jsonl. Inspect scripts/text_filters.py and config/categories.yaml to understand the cleaning and keyword weights. Apply clean_text from scripts/text_filters.py to every record before any matching.

Classification and scoring rules:
- For each record, compute a category_score for every category using case-insensitive matching over cleaned text. Single-word keywords must match whole words; multi-word phrases must match exact substrings.
- category_score(category) = sum(weights of positive keywords found in that category) minus sum(weights of negative keywords found in that category).
- Count each keyword at most once per record per category, even if it appears multiple times. If a keyword exists in more than one category and appears in the text, count it once in each applicable category.
- Assigned_category = the category with the highest category_score. If all category_scores are 0, set Assigned_category to "Uncategorized". If there is a tie for highest score, break ties by lexicographic order of the category name.
- Satisfaction_score (record-level) = sum of all positive keyword weights across all categories minus sum of all negative keyword weights across all categories, following the same matching rules.

Deliverables (save exactly to these paths):
1) outputs/segment_classification.csv with columns:
   - respondent_id
   - source_file (either "input/transcripts_round1.jsonl" or "input/transcripts_round2.jsonl")
   - assigned_category
   - satisfaction_score (integer)
   - matched_positive (semicolon-separated list of keyword strings, using the exact keys from config; empty if none)
   - matched_negative (semicolon-separated list of keyword strings, using the exact keys from config; empty if none)

2) outputs/top_segments_ranked.csv containing the top 20 records ranked by satisfaction_score descending. Columns:
   - respondent_id
   - source_file
   - assigned_category
   - satisfaction_score
   - text_cleaned (the cleaned text as returned by clean_text)

3) outputs/rewrite_top10.txt: For the 10 records with satisfaction_score < 0 that have the lowest scores (most negative), rewrite each original text into a neutral, professional tone, preserve the core issue, and cap each rewritten line at 160 characters. Output one line per record formatted as: "respondent_id: rewritten_text".

Notes:
- Ensure your matching and scores are consistent with config/categories.yaml and the cleaning logic in scripts/text_filters.py. Do not modify scripts/text_filters.py.
- Use only the provided files; no Internet access.
- Include both rounds in all aggregations and rankings. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
